result:
1. spoofable XFF key — `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']` read direct, Section 2 rule 2 + 2025-09 SEV1 repeat. Attacker rotates XFF header per request, bypasses per-IP limit, brute-forces auth. Replace with `trustedClientIp(req)` from `src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts`; verify via lint rule `no-direct-xff-read` + integration test rotating XFF sees single counter.
2. unbounded Redis key — `INCR` without `EXPIRE`, Section 4 + 2025-11 SEV2 repeat. Key lives forever, Redis OOM under traffic spike, edge offline. Wrap in `multi().incr(key).expire(key, 90).exec()`; verify via `no-unbounded-redis-key` lint + assert TTL>0 post-INCR in integration test.
3. missing route + method + region prefix — key is `rl:{ip}` only, Section 4 key format + 2025-12 SEV2 repeat. Cross-route global limit collision; one busy user throttled across regions. Use `rl:{region}:{ip}:{routePrefix}:{minuteBucket}`; verify by hitting two routes and confirming separate counters in `redis-cli KEYS rl:*`.
4. no minute bucket — no sliding window, counter never resets per window. Limit becomes lifetime-of-key cap, diverges from 60s window contract. Add `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)` segment per Section 4; verify counter resets across bucket boundary in test.
5. no limit check / 429 response — `incr` result unused, no threshold comparison, no RFC 7807 body. Limiter is a no-op; metric `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action=throttled}` never increments. Compare `count > limit` from `config/rate_limits.json`, return `problem+json` with `retry_after`; verify 429 fires at limit+1 in test.
6. no error path / metric — bare `await redis.incr`; Redis transient error throws, middleware 500s instead of fail-open or retry. Wrap in try/catch, emit `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action=errored}`, retry via `src/util/retry.ts`; verify by killing Redis in integration test — edge should degrade not crash.
